name: comprehensive-review-pr-enhance
description: You are a PR optimization expert specializing in creating high-quality pull requests that facilitate efficient code reviews. Generate comprehensive PR descriptions, automate review processes, and ensure PRs follow best practices for clarity, size, and reviewability.
capabilities: Analyze git diffs to extract change summaries and statistics
Generate structured PR descriptions with what changed and why
Create context-aware review checklists based on file types
Produce risk assessments with mitigation strategies
Generate test coverage reports comparing before and after states
Suggest logical PR splits for large changesets
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All static analysis findings evaluated as false positives. The skill contains Python code examples and templates within markdown documentation files, not executable code. The subprocess calls and shell commands exist only as illustrative examples in code blocks. No actual command execution, network access, or security risks present. Safe for publication.
valueStatement: Struggling with unclear PR descriptions and slow code reviews? This skill creates comprehensive PR documentation with change summaries, review checklists, risk assessments, and test coverage reports to accelerate the review process.
userTitle: Generate professional pull requests with detailed descriptions
limitations: Requires access to git history and diff information to function
Code examples are documentation templates, not executable tools
Does not directly integrate with GitHub or GitLab APIs
Risk assessments are heuristic-based and should be manually verified

useCases.targetUser: Developer submitting PRs to open source repositories
useCases.title: Open Source Contributor
useCases.description: Generate well-structured PR descriptions for contributions to large projects with established review processes and checklist requirements.

useCases.targetUser: Team lead managing code review workflows
useCases.title: Engineering Team Lead
useCases.description: Standardize PR quality across the team by generating consistent descriptions, review checklists, and risk documentation for all merges.

useCases.title: Solo Developer
useCases.description: Create thorough documentation for personal projects to track changes over time and facilitate future maintenance or collaboration.

promptTemplates.title: Basic PR Summary
promptTemplates.prompt: Review the changes in my current branch compared to main and write a concise PR summary explaining what changed and why.
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick PR for a small bug fix or minor feature

promptTemplates.title: Detailed PR Description
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze my PR diff and generate a comprehensive description including: executive summary, what changed, testing performed, risks, breaking changes, and a review checklist tailored to the file types modified.
promptTemplates.scenario: Standard feature PR requiring thorough documentation

promptTemplates.title: Large PR Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: My PR has many changes. Analyze the diff to identify logical groupings and suggest how to split this into smaller, reviewable PRs. For each suggested split, explain the scope and which files belong together.
promptTemplates.scenario: Refactoring or large feature that may need splitting

promptTemplates.title: Full PR Package
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a complete PR package: analyze the changes, generate a detailed description with all sections, produce a risk assessment with mitigation strategies, create a test coverage report, suggest any architectural diagrams needed, and provide context-aware review checklists for each category of change.
promptTemplates.scenario: Critical or complex PR requiring comprehensive documentation

bestPractices: Keep PRs focused on a single logical change to simplify review
Include before and after examples for behavioral changes
Test your changes locally and document the test results in the PR
antiPatterns: Submitting PRs with hundreds of files changed without breaking them down
Writing vague descriptions like 'fixed stuff' or 'misc improvements'
Leaving TODO comments or debugging statements in the final code

faq.question: What information do I need to provide for this skill to work?
faq.answer: Provide your current branch name, base branch (usually main), and optionally the git diff or commit messages. The skill will analyze these to generate comprehensive PR documentation.

faq.question: Can this skill directly create PRs on GitHub?
faq.answer: No, this skill generates PR descriptions and documentation. You will need to copy the output and create the PR manually through GitHub, GitLab, or your preferred platform.

faq.question: How does the risk assessment work?
faq.answer: The skill evaluates factors like PR size, complexity, files affected, test coverage, and security-sensitive changes to calculate a risk score. Always verify the assessment with your own judgment.

faq.question: What makes a PR easier to review?
faq.answer: Clear summaries, focused scope, descriptive commit messages, relevant test coverage, and explicit callouts of breaking changes or architectural decisions help reviewers understand and approve changes faster.

faq.question: Should I use this for every PR?
faq.answer: This skill is most valuable for medium to large PRs or when contributing to projects with formal review processes. Small typo fixes may not need comprehensive documentation.

faq.question: Can I customize the generated templates?
faq.answer: Yes, the output is meant to be a starting point. Modify the generated content to match your project's conventions and add any context specific to your changes.
